description_long: This obelisk is a tall, slender, four-sided monument with a sharply tapering shaft that culminates in a pointed tip. The structure is mounted on a broad, square base that features multiple stepped layers, giving it a sense of stability and prominence. The surface appears to be made of a uniform, gray stone or concrete material, with clean, angular lines and no visible ornamentation or inscriptions. The base is solid and geometric, with a slightly raised platform and beveled edges that transition smoothly into the vertical shaft. The overall design is minimalist and functional, evoking classical or commemorative urban monuments. The object is imposing and would serve as a focal point in a public square, park, or plaza.
description: A tall, gray, four-sided obelisk with a pointed tip, mounted on a stepped square base. The design is minimalist and solid, with no visible ornamentation or inscriptions.
description_view_0: Front view shows the obelisk's full height, square base, and sharply defined shaft with clean lines.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, emphasizing the symmetry and uniformity of the shaft and base.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same stepped base and vertical shaft visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the symmetrical, unadorned shaft and base.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, square platform with multiple stepped layers, beveled edges, and a solid, geometric structure. It appears to be made of the same gray material as the shaft, with no decorative features.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is sharply pointed, forming a clean pyramid shape with no unique features or decorations.
